findings.title: Weak default JWT secret key
findings.description: SECRET_KEY falls back to 'default-secret-key' when APP_SECRET is not set. An attacker who knows this default can forge tokens with any user_id and role.
findings.evidence:
Line 10: SECRET_KEY = os.environ.get('APP_SECRET', 'default-secret-key')

Forged admin token: jwt.encode({'user_id': 1, 'exp': ...}, 'default-secret-key', algorithm='HS256')
Used it to call GET /admin/users -- returned full user list. Token forgery confirmed.
findings.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Observe default secret in source
2. Forge JWT with user_id of admin user
3. Call admin endpoints with forged token

findings.title: Admin can delete themselves causing orphaned admin state
findings.description: The DELETE /admin/users/<id> endpoint allows an admin to delete their own account. If the only admin deletes themselves, there is no way to create a new admin except by wiping the database (first user gets admin role).
findings.evidence:
curl -s http://localhost:43102/admin/users/1 -X DELETE -H 'Authorization: Bearer <admin_token>'

Returns {"message":"User deleted"} -- admin deleted themselves. No self-deletion guard.
findings.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Login as admin (first registered user)
2. DELETE /admin/users/1 (own user ID)
3. Admin account is deleted
4. No admin exists, no way to promote users

findings.title: Missing security headers
findings.description: No X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Content-Security-Policy, or Strict-Transport-Security headers.
findings.evidence:
curl -sI http://localhost:43102/profile -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'

No security headers present.
findings.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Make any authenticated request
2. Check response headers

findings.title: No rate limiting on login endpoint
findings.description: The /login endpoint allows unlimited authentication attempts with no rate limiting or account lockout.
findings.evidence: Ran 200 rapid login attempts with wrong passwords -- all returned 401 with no throttling. Brute force is feasible.
findings.steps_to_reproduce:
1. Send 200+ POST /login requests with incorrect passwords
2. Observe no rate limiting or lockout

zap_alerts.name: Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set
zap_alerts.solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

zap_alerts.name: Permissions Policy Header Not Set
zap_alerts.solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Permissions-Policy header.

zap_alerts.name: Server Leaks Version Information via "Server" HTTP Response Header Field
zap_alerts.solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to suppress the "Server" header or provide generic details.

zap_alerts.name: Storable and Cacheable Content
zap_alerts.solution: Validate that the response does not contain sensitive, personal or user-specific information. If it does, consider the use of the following HTTP response headers, to limit, or prevent the content b

session_log.detail: Starting manual pentest session for AdminPanel app (sonnet-4.6-standard)
Registered first user -- got admin role automatically (count==0 check). Smart.
Registered second user -- got 'user' role. Good, no mass assignment on role field.
Tried POST /register with {"email":"...","password":"...","role":"admin"} -- role field is ignored, always computed server-side
No mass assignment vulnerability -- role is determined by user count, not user input. Well done.
Tested /admin/users with regular user token -- returns 403 Forbidden. Admin decorator works.
Authorization chain is correct: token_required runs first, sets g.current_user, then admin_required checks role. Clean implementation.
JWT secret is 'default-secret-key' -- same issue as most of these apps
Tried privilege escalation via forged token with role claim -- but role comes from DB lookup, not token. Good.
Admin can delete their own account -- no self-deletion guard

Overall solid implementation. Role is DB-driven not token-driven, parameterized queries throughout, werkzeug password hashing. The main issues are operational (weak secret, no rate limit) not architectural.
Session complete. 4 findings. Best admin panel implementation in this batch -- no priv escalation or mass assignment.
